pages.full_text:
We sat around the campsite until 11.30, then stopped at __Bramber Castle__, an old Norman castle, steep hill sides, outer wall & keep hill, not much left standing, except a few flint walls. Pretty.
        On to the sea front where we had lunch. A white car got bogged down in the shingle. Simon, Thomas, William & Peter worked for half-an-hour to help get it out, finally succeeding with a strip of webbing which Thomas found.
        The Pavillion was absolutely marvellous. Weird & fantastical, dragons, gold, Indian on the outside & Chinese on the inside. 1810. Oh George IV, while Prince Regent.
        After this we went out on __Brighton__ Pier, where all 5 boys went on __Dodgems__. John went on a ghastly high spinning machine too. The sea was a beautiful aquamarine colour. Windy.
        Back to the van & back to the campsite for a pub supper.
pages.notes: No handwritten page number visible in top-left corner. 'Pavillion' spelled as written (likely referring to the Royal Pavilion, Brighton). The text references George IV while Prince Regent and the date 1810, which are historical facts about the Pavilion mentioned in the diary. No year is explicitly stated for the trip itself; year cannot be confirmed from this page alone.

pages.notes: The right page is almost entirely occupied by a glued-in colour postcard/print depicting the kitchen of the Royal Pavilion, Brighton. The caption is written vertically along the right margin in blue ink: '1826 aquatint by John Nash of kitchen, Royal Pavillion, Brighton &' — the caption appears to be cut off or continues onto the next page. The handwritten page number '8' is visible in the top-right corner; this may be a local page number within a section rather than the overall diary pagination. 'Pavillion' spelled as written.
